data.prompt:
You are Archon Horizon's Horizon agent. You turn the blueprints into checked Lean: you pick the most valuable next piece of formalization and carry it as far as you can in one session. You receive the same workspace context Ground saw — roadmap, inbox, memory, blueprint summary, and any Ground orientation — but you choose the strategy from the live Lean state.

You have broad freedom at the workspace level. You may change the route, fill infrastructure gaps, refactor proof APIs, edit blueprint material, update roadmap comments, retrieve references, delegate to subagents, and write local helper scripts/tools when that helps. The one hard rule is hygiene: stay within the write scope and shared `references/`, keep changes semantic, and leave no stray scratch files. Prefer ambitious progress over defensive avoidance.

Operational norms:
- Use the Lean LSP MCP for tight proof feedback, then verify with the narrowest faithful Lean/lake check.
- Use skills as needed for Lean checks, inbox coordination, commits, references, and subagents.
- Use `"$HORIZON_BIN"` for Horizon CLI actions; write inbox/task/roadmap comments with `--author horizon`.
- Commit coherent progress yourself with `"$HORIZON_BIN" commit -m "<math-first message>" <files>`.
- Record durable dead ends as memory and explain important route changes in your final report.

# Skills
Invoke the Horizon CLI via the `$HORIZON_BIN` env var (an absolute path), e.g. `"$HORIZON_BIN" inbox list --json` — a bare `horizon` may not be on your shell's PATH.
Most `horizon` commands accept `--json`; use it for AI-friendly output when reading state or ids.
Capability know-how lives in skill files under the workspace `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (Claude Code surfaces them automatically; on other engines read the files directly). Consult the relevant one before acting (exact inbox verbs, lean checks, blueprint conventions, the dependency DAG):
- blueprint-conventions — The LaTeX-subset blueprint format and house style — environments, \uses/\lean/\leanok/\mathlibok/\notready, the math-purity rule, and complete (not sketched) proofs.
- horizon-commit — Record your own work in the workspace ledger with semantic commits via `horizon commit` — small, meaningful, math-first messages; safe under concurrent runs.
- horizon-inbox — Read and act on the Archon Horizon inbox — list/filter agent-ready items, understand labels/kinds, comment, archive, create, and message other projects via the `horizon inbox` CLI.
- horizon-waiting — How to wait for slow work (builds, subagents, monitors) inside a one-shot session — block in the foreground and get the result before you report; you are NEVER re-invoked on completion.
- lean-check — Build and check Lean in this workspace and read the errors — use Lean LSP MCP for fast proof-writing feedback, then run faithful kernel verification with `lake build` / `lake env lean`.
- leandag — Consult and refresh the blueprint dependency DAG (leandag) — declaration dependencies, cones, proved status, isolated/dangling nodes, verified union/intersection across projects — via `horizon leandag` and the cached DAG files.
- leansearch — Find existing lemmas/definitions before proving — the local `horizon search` index plus the Lean LSP MCP search tools (LeanSearch/Loogle/state-search/hammer-premise). Reach for search before writing any nontrivial proof; the premise you need often already exists in mathlib.
- project-git — How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace — root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
- references — The shared workspace-root references/ library and its manifest.yaml — how to find, read, and cite original sources with \source{...}. Read the source before you cite it (never speculate); anchor TeX sources by slug, and for PDF-only sources extract the LaTeX first (vision, never OCR).
- subagents — How the Ground agent delegates to native subagents to divide review/upkeep work — spawning by name, read-only enforcement, model tiers, and the dynamic descriptor roster.

Workspace: JacobianChallenge
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge at MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Write scope — you may edit ONLY paths matching these globs; treat everything else as read-only:
- MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/**
- references/**
Read scope: the entire workspace (cross-project reads are allowed).
Log/artifact directory: .archon-horizon/runs/0010/sessions/0084-horizon-T12

# Subagents
You have native subagents installed for your engine, compiled at run start from the descriptors in `.archon-horizon/subagents/<name>.md` (Claude reads `.claude/agents/`, Codex reads `.codex/agents/`):
- blueprint — Own one assigned blueprint slice end-to-end — keep it pure math with complete proofs, cite references with \source{...} anchors, hold a 1-to-1 correspondence with the Lean, and make its dependency cone finite and clean.
- debug — Diagnose and repair workspace setup/build/environment failures during a run — missing toolchain, broken lakefile, MCP/config/env problems, obscure non-Lean-syntax errors — applying safe fixes or reporting a precise diagnosis.
- janitor — Workspace-hygiene auditor and tidier — keeps the workspace and each subproject navigable, READMEs/roadmap concise and current, files findable, and the inbox from overflowing (capping memory tags, closing what's done).
- page-transcriber — Transcribe a small PDF page range (max 5 pages) into faithful per-page LaTeX using AI VISION on rendered page images (never OCR), each page self-contained with the operators/macros it declares.
- reference-retriever — Fetch original math references into workspace-root references/, register them in references/manifest.yaml, and when needed transcribe PDF page ranges into page-level LaTeX files for blueprint \source{...} anchors.
- work-reviewer [read-only] — Fresh-context review of the previous Horizon agent's work on a task — starts from its git diff, its report, and the task it was given, but is free to dig deeper — then judges whether the work is converging and files concise inbox issues/memory for stuck points and errors.

Delegate by spawning a subagent **by name** through your engine's own subagent mechanism, giving it a focused directive: the slice/scope and the project it applies to. Spawn several in parallel when the work divides cleanly, then wait for and reconcile their reports. Read-only subagents are sandboxed off source edits and report through the `horizon inbox` CLI. You decide whether a subagent fits — use them when they help, skip them when they don't. Each subagent's descriptor pins its own model, usually a cheaper one for mechanical work: prefer delegating routine checks (diff review, reference lookup, lint) to those cheaper subagents and keep your own (more expensive) context for the hard reasoning. To add or change a subagent, edit its descriptor under `.archon-horizon/subagents/` — it recompiles on the next run.

# Task focus
Task: T12
Title: Picard scheme representability (FGA) — Pic_{C/k} representable, locally of finite type
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Suggested scope: projects=Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge; files=AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/GrassmannianQuot.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FlatteningStratification.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean
References: roadmap=AJC.picrep

Prove **representability of the relative Picard functor by a scheme** (FGA / Grothendieck FGA 232 / Theorem 4.8 in Kleiman) — roadmap cone `AJC.picrep`. For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve $C/k$ with a $k$-rational point, the relative Picard functor is representable by a $k$-scheme locally of finite type:

$$\underline{\mathrm{Pic}}_{C/k}\ \text{representable by a scheme, locally of finite type}.$$

Now that most of the necessary architecture is already made, `GrassmannianQuot`, `FlatteningStratification`, `GenericFlatnessGeometric`, etc, you should already commit to the hard sorries and close the representability task.

# Roadmap slice
- AJC.albanese [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Albanese / abelian-variety leg (merges from Albanese) - **Albanese / abelian-variety leg.** The Albanese universal property of $\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ and the rational-map-extension machinery, developed in `Albanese`, merge back here to supply the abelian-variety structure feeding `AJC.jacobian`.

- AJC.cech [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Čech R^i f_* engine (merged) - **Čech higher-direct-image engine (merged, sorry-free).**
$$H^{i}\big(\check{C}^{\bullet}(\mathfrak{U},\mathcal{F})\big)\;\cong\;R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\qquad(f\ \text{separated quasi-compact, finite affine cover}).$$
`cech_computes_higherDirectImage` + `pushPull` functoriality, proved in `Cech-Cohomology` and merged into AJC — axiom-clean. The engine cone is sorry-free on Lean v4.31 (the former migration-interim sorries closed by T8). Residual base-change sorries live in `AJC.fbc`, not here.

- AJC.fbc [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Flat base change (Stacks 02KH) - **Flat base change (Stacks 02KH), Čech route.** For flat $g$:
$$g^{*}R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;R^{i}f'_{*}\,g'^{*}\mathcal{F}.$$
Homology machinery sorry-free (separated, no spectral sequence). **02KG affine-reduction heart closed**: the per-$\sigma$ heart `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChange` (ring-pushout carve + corner-sections tensor rewrite + the affine brick `affinePushforwardPullbackBaseChange`), together with `openImmersion_beckChevalley`/`bareBC`, the affine essential-image node (`Cohomology/AffinePushPullEssImage.lean`, `twisted_cech_nerve_per_sigma`), the RHS tilde leaf `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChanged_pushforward_iso_tilde`, and arbitrary-morphism pullback quasi-coherence (`Cohomology/PullbackQuasicoherent.lean`, 01BG) are sorry-free and axiom-clean. The superseded canonical-mate route was pruned; `Cohomology/FlatBaseChange.lean` is now sorry-free. Residual toward `cechComplex_baseChange_iso` — **3 leaf sorries** (all in `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean`): the two cosimplicial `naturality` fields (`cech_pushforward_baseChange_natIso`, `twisted_cech_nerve_iso`) and `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (flat $g^{*}$ left-exact — **not** an adjointness freebie, see `I-0076`). See task **T2** and memory `I-0083`.

- AJC.grquot [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged) - **Relative Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged, sorry-free).**
$$\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)(T)\;\cong\;\{\,V_{T}\twoheadrightarrow Q\ \text{locally free rank }d\,\}\quad(\texttt{Grassmannian.represents}).$$
Plus the SNAP section graded ring/module (Stacks 01CV) and glue-descent atlas. Merged from `GR-Quot-Closure`.

- AJC.jacobian [pending/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Jacobian = Albanese (top goal) - **North star.** For a smooth proper geometrically-irreducible curve $C/k$, the Jacobian is an abelian variety representing the degree-$0$ relative Picard functor and realizing the Albanese property:
$$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}(T)\;=\;\ker\!\big(\mathrm{Pic}(C\times_{k}T)\to\mathrm{Pic}(C)\big),\qquad \dim_{k}\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}=g.$$
Smooth of relative dimension $g$, proper, geometrically irreducible (`exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`).

- AJC.linebundle [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Line-bundle comparison-iso / Pic^sharp group (A.1.c.sub) - **Line-bundle comparison-iso substrate (A.1.c.sub).** Gives $\mathrm{Pic}^{\sharp}_{C/k}$ its abelian-group law via the tensor/dual comparison
$$\mathcal{L}\otimes_{\mathcal{O}_C}\mathcal{L}^{-1}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;\mathcal{O}_{C}.$$
Finished in `Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso`; merges back into AJC via task **T1** (closes the 5 residual `TensorObjSubstrate`/`PullbackTensorComp` sorries).

- AJC.pic0av [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Pic^0 is an abelian variety (A.3) - **$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ is an abelian variety (A.3).** Kleiman §5 tangent-space identification, then structure:
$$T_{e}\,\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}\;\cong\;H^{1}(C,\mathcal{O}_{C}),\qquad \dim=g.$$
Remaining: the $k$-linear comparison $\operatorname{Dual}_k(\mathfrak{m}_e/\mathfrak{m}_e^{2})\cong H^{1}(C,\mathcal O_C)$ (`tangentSpaceIso`); then un-invert `smooth` and `proper` (Chevalley–Rosenlicht) and geometric irreducibility.

- AJC.picrep [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Picard scheme representability cone - **Picard scheme representability (FGA).** The relative Picard functor is representable by a scheme locally of finite type:
$$\underline{\mathrm{Pic}}_{C/k}\ \text{representable};\qquad \mathrm{Quot},\ \mathrm{Grass},\ \text{flattening stratification as inputs}.$$
Cone: `FGAPicRepresentability`, `QuotScheme` endgame, `FlatteningStratification`, `IdentityComponent`.

- AJC.substrate [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Foundational substrate (Kähler, rigidity, Abel–Jacobi) - **Foundational substrate (sorry-free).** Kähler differentials / cotangent ($\Omega_{C/k}$, cotangent iso, chart algebra), the rigidity lemma, genus theory, Abel–Jacobi scaffolding, and line-bundle coherence/pullback (`RelPicFunctor`, `RelativeSpec`).

# Memory
- **tangentSpaceIso (A.3.iii) is NOT directly provable — critical path runs through the relative Pic functor, and a naive FGA rewire is a FALSE-axiom trap.**

- `Pic0.tangentSpaceIso` (`Pic0AbelianVariety.lean`) cannot be proven against the current foundation: `picSharp` in `FGAPicRepresentability.lean` is `Classical.choice` of a `⟨sorry⟩` `Nonempty` — a fully opaque functor. No H¹ comparison can attach to it. Do not re-probe the sorry directly.
- **Critical path** (in order): (1) ~~real relative Pic presheaf~~ DONE session 0015 (`PicSharp.relPresheaf` in `RelPicFunctor.lean`, `H_T`-coset carrier, axiom-clean); (2) étale sheafification target — Mathlib v4.31 has NO étale Grothendieck topology on schemes; either parametrize or use Kleiman §2 Thm 2.5: for C/k with a **k-rational section**, `Pic^♯(T) → Pic^♯_ét(T)` is already bijective, so representability can be stated against `relPresheaf` directly under a section hypothesis; (3) rewire FGA `picSharp` := (sheafified) relative functor — universe bump `Type u → Type (u+1)` in `HasPicSharp`/`HasPicScheme` is viable (`Functor.RepresentableBy` is universe-polymorphic; `Equiv` is cross-universe); (4) `Pic(C_ε) ≅ H¹(C_ε, O^×)` cocycle classification + truncated exponential split sequence `0 → O_C → O^×_{C_ε} → O^×_C → 1` for the kernel = `H¹(C,O_C)` computation (Kleiman Thm 5.11) — none of this exists yet, largest remaining block.
- **TRAP**: wiring `picSharp := PicSharp.presheaf` (the absolute functor, iso-class carrier) would make `PicSharpRepresentable`'s sorry a mathematically FALSE statement (absolute `Pic(C×T)` is not representable — it differs from `Hom(T, Pic)` by `Pic(T)`). Any rewire must use the relative (and in general sheafified) functor.
- Proof-pattern notes: the `Quotient.lift`/`Quotient.sound` + iso-chain style (pullbackCongr/pullbackId/pullbackComp `.app`, D4′ `pullbackTensorIsoOfLocallyTrivial`, `pullback_pullback_eq`) compiled essentially first-try — no v4.31 transparency walls in this corner. `pullback.lift_fst/snd` are NOT simp (only `@[reassoc]`); pass them + their `_assoc` forms to `simp` explicitly after `pullback.hom_ext`.
- Milne 3.1/3.2 extension chain: "CodimOneFree ⇒ extension to a complete target" is FALSE — never re-add it; the honest chain and the reusable Mathlib extension recipe.

- **Do not reintroduce** an `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`-style statement (codim-1-free rational map to a complete variety extends): counterexample $\mathbb P^2 \dashrightarrow \mathbb P^1$ away from a point (indeterminacy = one codim-2 point, no regular extension; blow-up needed). Removed in run-0006 session 0015 after its `sorry` sat unclosable for ~25 iterations. A Weil-style codim-≥2 extension is true only for **group-variety** targets, and Milne's route never needs it.
- **Honest chain** (all in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` + `Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`): Milne 3.1 = `indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete` (proved, valuative); Milne 3.3 (`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`, the only remaining sorry) must assert the codim-1 witness lies **in $Z(f)$** (without that conjunct the disjunct is nearly vacuous and cannot force emptiness); 3.3 + 3.1 ⇒ $Z(f)=\emptyset$ (`av_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`); then extension. **Stacks 0AVF / Auslander–Buchsbaum depth-≥2 is NOT needed anywhere in this chain** (supersedes older notes listing 0AVF as a Milne-3.1 blocker).
- **Recipe: extension from empty indeterminacy** (`existsUnique_hom_of_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`, reduced X / separated Y): witness `X.topIso.inv ≫ X.homOfLE hdom.ge ≫ f.toPartialMap.hom` where `hdom : f.toPartialMap.domain = ⊤` (`toPartialMap.domain = f.domain` is `rfl`); rational-class equality via `PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mpr ⟨⊤, …⟩` + `simp only [PartialMap.restrict_hom, Scheme.Hom.toPartialMap, Scheme.topIso_hom, Scheme.ι_toIso_inv_assoc, Scheme.homOfLE_homOfLE_assoc]`; uniqueness via `hom_ext_of_toRationalMap_eq` (= `PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mp` + `Opens.isDominant_ι` + `ext_of_isDominant`, positional arg — the parser rejects `(ι := …)` named form here). Both lemmas need `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false` BEFORE the docstring (`(restrict …).domain` / `Hom.toPartialMap` defeq walls).
- **Standing lesson**: before attacking a long-lived typed `sorry`, sanity-check the statement for truth (hunt small counterexamples); a false pinned statement silently blocks a whole cone and can smuggle wrong `\uses` (here it dragged a phantom `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay`/0AVF dependency into the blueprint for ~30 iterations).
- **FGA picSharp rewire LANDED (run 0008): route-(b) conventions + two standing caveats.**

State after the rewire (`Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean`, **4 real `sorry`s**, all TRUE statements — Ground-verified run 0008 reconcile):

- `picSharp C := PicSharp.relPresheaf C ⋙ forget AddCommGrpCat` (`Type (u+1)`-valued, REAL). `Scheme.HasRationalPoint C` = Nonempty section subtype of `C.hom`; the ONE genuine FGA sorry is `instHasPicScheme [HasRationalPoint C]`. The other three are typed-sorry carriers: `PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType` (Kleiman §4 `th:main`(1), true under the rational point) and the pre-existing Quot-blocked `HasDivFunctor` / `HasAbelMap`.
- `instPicSharpRepresentable` proved by `choose_spec`; `groupSchemeStructure : CommGrpObj (PicScheme C)` proved via `CommGrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` + a `Multiplicative.ofAdd` NatIso (`picSharpCommGrpForgetIso`, naturality is `rfl` — no defeq walls anywhere in this file).
- Consumers: quantify over `[HasPicScheme C]` (synthesized from `[HasRationalPoint C]`) — `degree`/`kPoints_iff_kerDegree` in `IdentityComponent.lean` now carry it. `Pic0Scheme C := GroupScheme.IdentityComponent (PicScheme C)` is now a real def (axiom-clean), gated on `PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType`.

**Caveat 1 — pointless curves:** `JacobianWitness` (`Jacobian.lean`) requires the abelian variety $J$ to exist UNconditionally, but route (b) only builds `PicScheme` for pointed $C$. For a genus-$\ge 1$ curve without a rational point the eventual `picardJacobianWitness` proof needs Galois/étale descent from a finite extension where $C$ gains a point (or a pointed restatement of the north star). Not blocking anything now — record before anyone tries to close `picardJacobianWitness` directly.

**Caveat 2 — `smoothProperQuotient` statement is WEAKER than Kleiman lm:qt and unprovable as stated:** Mathlib v4.31 has NO quasi-projectivity vocabulary, so hypothesis (2) of lm:qt is missing from the Lean statement; without it the statement is FALSE (Hironaka-type free ℤ/2 action on a smooth proper non-projective 3-fold: smooth proper equivalence relation, étale-quotient sheaf a non-scheme algebraic space). The former GLOBAL `instHasSmoothProperQuotient ⟨sorry⟩` (every presheaf representable!) was deleted this run (Ground-confirmed gone); the class is a use-site hypothesis only. Do NOT reintroduce a global instance, and do NOT try to prove the theorem as stated.
- **Stacks 01BG general pullback-qcoh CLOSED + FBC RHS tilde leaf CLOSED (run 0003 session 0013, T2) — supersedes all "general-morphism pullback qcoh is absent / only in sorry-tainted QuotScheme" notes.**

- `Cohomology/PullbackQuasicoherent.lean` (new, ~185 LOC, axiom-clean): `pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom (g : Y ⟶ X)` for ARBITRARY scheme morphisms. Recipe: localize the pullback, not the presentation — per cover member `A`, `(g^*F)|_{g⁻¹A} ≅ (g.resLE A W)^*(F|_A)` via `pullbackComp`+`pullbackCongr (resLE_comp_ι g le_rfl)`+`restrictFunctorIsoPullback`, transport the global presentation of `F|_A` by `SheafOfModules.Presentation.map` (15-underscore @-application, copy `presentationRestrictSliceOfOver`'s shape), then the CechTermAcyclic over-bridge tail (`modulesOverOpensEquivalence` / `overOpensIsoRestrict` / `overOpensFunctorUnitIso` / counit) and `IsQuasicoherent.of_coversTop` on the preimage cover.
- Enablers now available: `opensMap_final` (`(Opens.map φ).Final` for ANY continuous map — structured-arrow posets are ⊔-directed, `Functor.final_of_isFiltered_structuredArrow`) and `pullbackUnitIso : g^*𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_Y` (Mathlib's `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` iso under `[F.Final]`). Gotcha: re-synthesis of `IsIso (pullbackObjUnitToUnit …)` at USE sites can fail (defeq wall in the instance-arg unification) — pass the instance explicitly via `@asIso _ _ _ _ _ (pullbackObjUnitToUnit_isIso_hom g)`.
- FBC leaf `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChanged_pushforward_iso_tilde` (622-line sorry) closed as literally `(pushforward f').mapIso (twisted_cech_nerve_per_sigma …) ≪≫ pushPullObj_coverInter_pushforward_iso_tilde f' 𝒰' (g'^*F) (pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom g' F hF) σ` — the def had to MOVE below `twisted_cech_nerve_per_sigma` in the file. FBC leaves 5 → 4.
- Iteration cost: `lake env lean` on a file importing Mathlib+`CechTermAcyclic` ≈ 4 min under load ~65; the 2-module `lake build` (new file + `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional`) ≈ 25 min.
- **`pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (FBC leaf, `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean:186`) is NOT a cheap adjointness win — Ground's recommendation had the variance backwards. Route analysis (session 0013), do not re-derive.**

- The recommendation claimed "flat `g^*` is a left adjoint, so left-exactness follows from adjointness" — **false**: left adjoints preserve COLIMITS. Left-exactness of flat pullback is genuine flatness content, checked on stalks.
- No dodge exists at the presheaf level: `PresheafOfModules.pullback` is a bare `leftAdjoint` with no pointwise description; the sections-level formula `colim_{U ⊇ g(U')} M(U) ⊗_{O(U)} O(U')` is only RIGHT-exact (the ring maps are not flat — flatness lives on stalks). Sheafification + `SheafOfModules.forget` both preserve finite limits (Mathlib), so the irreducible core really is stalk-level.
- **Viable honest route** (est. 400–800 LOC of new infra): (1) combinators exist — `Functor.preservesFiniteLimits_tfae` / `preservesFiniteLimits_of_preservesKernels` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/ShortComplex/ExactFunctor.lean`); (2) Mathlib 2026 has module structure on stalks (`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Stalk.lean`, `PresheafOfModules.germ_smul`) and stalk⊣skyscraper at plain-category level (`Topology/Sheaves/Skyscraper.lean`), plus `mono_iff_stalk_mono`/`isIso_iff_stalkFunctor_map_iso` (`Topology/Sheaves/Stalks.lean`); (3) the missing brick is **stalk-of-pullback**: `stalkModule_x ∘ pullback g ≅ extendScalars(stalkMap) ∘ stalkModule_{g x}` — provable by adjoint uniqueness IF a module-level skyscraper right adjoint is built (pushforward of skyscraper = skyscraper at the image); then flat stalk maps (`AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.iff_flat_stalkMap`-adjacent, `RingHom.Flat` appLE definition) + `ModuleCat.preservesFiniteLimits_extendScalars_of_flat` (`ModuleCat/Descent.lean`) finish via kernel-comparison-on-stalks.

11

13	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the
14	Grothendieck–Altman–Kleiman Quot-scheme construction
15	`Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` — a projective `S`-scheme representing the functor of
16	`T`-flat coherent quotients of `E_T` on `X_T = X ×_S T` with Hilbert
17	polynomial `Φ` on every fiber — together with the in-project sub-build for
18	the Grassmannian *scheme* (Mathlib at the pinned commit carries only a
19	linear-algebra Grassmannian).

21	## Status (iter-176 Lane H file-skeleton — re-dispatch; §1 closed run 0011)

61	   functor: for a noetherian `S`, a projective `π : X ⟶ S`, a relatively

63	   `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` is representable by a projective `S`-scheme.
64	
65	6. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatBaseChangeCohomology` (theorem, ~10 LOC) — the
66	   **flat base-change theorem of cohomology** (Stacks tag 02KH): for a
67	   cartesian square with `g` flat and `f` quasi-compact quasi-separated,

74	## Note on type expressivity

76	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof",
77	each declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
78	
79	- `hilbertPolynomial` returns `Polynomial ℚ` keyed by `s : S`, not

144	/-! ## §1. Hilbert polynomial of a coherent sheaf
145	
146	The **Hilbert polynomial** `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.hilbertPolynomial` is a
147	**real definition** (no `sorry`), provided by
148	`AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.HilbertPolynomial` (imported above): the unique
149	`Φ_{F,s} ∈ ℚ[λ]` agreeing for `m ≫ 0` with the graded Hilbert function
150	`m ↦ dim_{κ(s)} Γ(X_s, F_s ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})` of the fibre section module
151	(`H⁰`-only encoding; agrees with the Euler-characteristic `χ(F(m))` form by
152	Serre vanishing for proper `X_s` and ample `L_s`).  See
153	`Scheme.hilbertPolynomial_eq_of_eventually` (uniqueness / well-definedness)

158	III.5.2). -/
159	
160	/-! ## §2. The Quot functor
161	
162	The Quot functor `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` sends an `S`-scheme `T ⟶ S` to the
163	set of equivalence classes `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` where
164	- `F` is a coherent sheaf on `X_T = X ×_S T` whose schematic support is
165	  proper over `T` and which is `T`-flat,
166	- `q : E_T ↠ F` is a surjective `O_{X_T}`-linear homomorphism,
167	- the fiberwise Hilbert polynomial of `F|_{X_t}` with respect to `L|_{X_t}`
168	  equals `Φ` at every `t ∈ T`.
169	
170	Two pairs `(F, q)` and `(F', q')` are equivalent iff `ker(q) = ker(q')`.
171	
172	The Hilbert scheme is the special case `E = O_X`:
173	`Hilb^{Φ,L}_{X/S} = Quot^{Φ,L}_{O_X/X/S}`.
174	
175	Blueprint reference: `def:quot_functor` (Nitsure §1; FGA Explained Ch. 5). -/
176	
177	/-- The **Quot functor** `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` of coherent quotients of `E`
178	on `X ×_S -` with Hilbert polynomial `Φ`.
179	
180	Encoded as a contravariant functor `(Over S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u`, sending an
181	`S`-scheme `T → S` (i.e. an object of `Over S`) to the set of
182	equivalence classes `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` of a `T`-flat coherent
183	sheaf `F` on `X ×_S T` with proper support and a surjection
184	`q : E_T ↠ F` whose fiberwise Hilbert polynomial is `Φ`, modulo
185	`ker(q) = ker(q')`. Functoriality is pullback of the quotient along
186	`X ×_S T' ⟶ X ×_S T`.
187	
188	iter-177+: the body packages the on-objects / on-morphisms data using the
189	`Scheme.Modules.pullback` bifunctor on the relative product
190	`X ×_S T`, with the equivalence relation `ker(q) = ker(q')` quotiented
191	out via `Setoid` / `Quotient`. For the iter-176 file-skeleton the body
192	is a typed `sorry`. -/
193	noncomputable def QuotFunctor {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
194	    (_π : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType _π] (_L _E : X.Modules)
195	    (_Φ : Polynomial ℚ) :
196	    (Over S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u :=
197	  sorry

200	
201	/-! ## §3. The Grassmannian scheme
202	
203	Since Mathlib carries no Grassmannian *scheme*, we encode it here as a
204	contravariant functor on `Over S` together with a representability
205	statement. The construction proceeds by gluing `binom(r, d)` affine
206	charts `U^I ≅ A^{d(r-d)}_S` along the Plücker cocycle, yielding a smooth
207	projective `S`-scheme `Gr_S(V, d)` of relative dimension `d(r-d)`,
208	equipped with a tautological rank-`d` quotient
209	`π* V ↠ U` and the Plücker closed embedding into `ℙ_S(⋀^d V)`.
210	
211	Blueprint references: `def:grassmannian_scheme`,
212	`thm:grassmannian_representable` (Nitsure §1 Exercise (2),
213	"Construction of Grassmannian"; FGA Explained Ch. 5). -/
214

216	
217	/-- The **Grassmannian functor** `Grass(V, d) : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` of
218	rank-`d` quotients of a locally free `O_S`-module `V` of rank `r ≥ d`.
219	
220	Encoded as the functor sending an `S`-scheme `T → S` to the set of
221	equivalence classes `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` with
222	`q : V_T ↠ F` a surjection of `O_T`-modules and `F` locally free of
223	rank `d`, modulo `ker(q) = ker(q')`. Concretely
224	`Grass(V, d) = Quot^{d, O_S}_{V/S/S}` (the Quot functor for `X = S`,
225	`E = V`, constant Hilbert polynomial `d`).
226

235	/-- **Representability of the Grassmannian.**
236	
237	For a noetherian scheme `S`, a locally free `O_S`-module `V` of rank `r`,
238	and `1 ≤ d ≤ r`, the Grassmannian functor `Grass(V, d)` of
239	`Grassmannian` is representable by a smooth projective `S`-scheme
240	`Gr_S(V, d) ⟶ S` of relative dimension `d(r-d)`, equipped with a
241	tautological rank-`d` quotient `π* V ↠ U`. The determinant line bundle
242	`det(U)` is relatively very ample, giving a Plücker closed embedding
243	`Gr_S(V, d) ↪ ℙ_S(⋀^d V)`.
244	
245	We package the conclusion as the existence of a representing
246	`Y : Over S` together with a `Functor.RepresentableBy Y` witness for
247	`Grassmannian V d`; the additional projective / smooth / Plücker
248	structure is implicit in the construction and is iter-177+ refinement
249	work (once the proof body lands).
250	
251	iter-177+: the body follows Nitsure §1 "Construction of Grassmannian":
252	glue the `binom(r, d)` affine charts `U^I ≅ A^{d(r-d)}_S` along the
253	Plücker cocycle, verify separatedness via the diagonal cut, verify
254	properness by the DVR valuative criterion, build the tautological
255	quotient `U`, exhibit the Plücker embedding via the determinant line
256	bundle. For the iter-176 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
257	theorem Grassmannian.representable {S : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
258	    (V : S.Modules) (d : ℕ) :
259	    ∃ (Y : Over S), Nonempty ((Grassmannian V d).RepresentableBy Y) := by
260	  sorry
261	
262	/-! ## §4. Representability of the Quot scheme
263	
264	Grothendieck–Altman–Kleiman: for a noetherian `S`, a projective
265	`π : X ⟶ S`, a relatively very ample `L` on `X`, a coherent
266	`E` on `X`, and `Φ ∈ ℚ[λ]`, the Quot functor `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` is
267	representable by a *projective* `S`-scheme.
268	
269	The proof has four steps (Nitsure §5):
270	1. **Boundedness** via Castelnuovo–Mumford `m`-regularity (uniform across
271	   fibers of `π` and across coherent quotients of `E_s` with Hilbert
272	   polynomial `Φ`).
273	2. **Grassmannian embedding**
274	   `α : Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S} ↪ Grass(W ⊗_{O_S} Sym^r V, Φ(r))`
275	   for `r ≥ m`, sending `⟨F, q⟩ ↦ ⟨(π_T)_* F(r), (π_T)_*(q(r))⟩`.
276	3. **Locally closed in Grassmannian** via the flattening stratification
277	   applied to the universal cokernel on the Grassmannian, producing the
278	   stratum `T_0^Φ`.
279	4. **Closed embedding** by the valuative criterion of properness for
280	   DVRs.
281	
282	The reduction to the universal case `X = ℙ(V)`, `E = π*W` is recorded as
283	`lem:quot_reduction_to_pi_star_W` in the blueprint chapter.
284	
285	Blueprint reference: `thm:quot_representable` (Nitsure §5; FGA Explained
286	Ch. 5; Grothendieck, FGA TDTE-IV). -/
287	
288	/-- **Representability of the Quot scheme** (Grothendieck, Altman–Kleiman).
289	
290	Let `S` be a noetherian scheme, `π : X ⟶ S` a projective morphism (here
291	encoded as a proper `LocallyOfFiniteType` morphism; the projectivity
292	upgrades once `IsProjective` lands in Mathlib), `L` a line bundle on `X`
293	(relatively very ample), `E` a coherent `O_X`-module, and
294	`Φ ∈ ℚ[λ]`. Then the Quot functor `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` of `QuotFunctor`
295	is representable by an `S`-scheme.
296	
297	We package the conclusion as the existence of `Q : Over S` together with
298	a `Functor.RepresentableBy Q` witness for `QuotFunctor π L E Φ`; the
299	*projectivity* of `Q ⟶ S` (and the universal quotient
300	`q^univ : π^*_Q E ↠ F^univ` on `X ×_S Q^{Φ,L}`) is implicit in the
301	construction (Plücker-embedded into a projective Grassmannian over `S`)
302	and is iter-177+ refinement work.
303	
304	iter-177+: the body follows the four-step Nitsure §5 proof
305	(boundedness ⟶ Grassmannian embedding ⟶ flattening stratification ⟶
306	valuative-criterion closed embedding); the sub-lemmas live in
307	`lem:quot_boundedness`, `lem:quot_alpha_injective`,
308	`lem:quot_valuative_criterion`, and the existential reduction in
309	`lem:quot_reduction_to_pi_star_W`. For the iter-176 file-skeleton the
310	body is a typed `sorry`. -/
311	theorem QuotScheme {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
312	    (π : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType π] [IsProper π]
313	    (L E : X.Modules) (Φ : Polynomial ℚ) :
314	    ∃ (Q : Over S), Nonempty ((QuotFunctor π L E Φ).RepresentableBy Q) := by
315	  sorry
316	
317	end Scheme
318	
319	/-! ## §5. Cohomology and base change
320	
321	The Quot construction uses cohomology-and-base-change in two places: the
322	boundedness step (Nitsure §5 "Use of m-Regularity") and the Grassmannian
323	embedding (Nitsure §5 "Embedding Quot into Grassmannian"). We record the
324	relevant statement as a named theorem so the Lean encoding can cite it
325	directly.
326	
327	The Stacks 02KH form is the statement for higher direct images
328	`R^i f_*` on quasi-coherent sheaves; for the iter-176 file-skeleton we
329	state the `i = 0` form on `Scheme.Modules`, which is the substantive
330	content of `lemma-flat-base-change-cohomology(ii)` of Stacks 02KH. The
331	`R^i` form is iter-177+ work after higher-direct-image infrastructure
332	is in scope.
333	
334	Blueprint reference: `thm:flat_base_change_cohomology` (Stacks 02KH). -/
335	
336	/-! ### Flat base change of cohomology (Stacks tag 02KH, `i = 0` form)
337	
338	Let
339	```
340	   g'
341	X' ───→ X
342	│       │
343	f'      f
344	↓       ↓
345	S' ───→ S
346	   g
347	```
348	be a cartesian square of schemes with `g` flat and `f` quasi-compact
349	quasi-separated. Let `F` be a sheaf of `O_X`-modules. Then the canonical
350	base-change map `g* (f_* F) ⟶ f'_* ((g')* F)` is an isomorphism in
351	`S'.Modules`.
352	
353	(The full Stacks 02KH statement covers all higher direct images
354	`R^i f_* F` for `i ≥ 0`; the `i = 0` form encoded here is the
355	substantive content of `lemma-flat-base-change-cohomology(ii)` of
356	Stacks 02KH, with the `i ≥ 1` form post-iter-177 work after the
357	higher-direct-image bifunctor lands.)
358	
359	iter-177 (Lane QS-FLAT): the body constructs the canonical base-change
360	natural transformation via the mate equivalence of the
361	`pullback ⊣ pushforward` adjunction (Mathlib's `mateEquiv` of
362	`Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction`), then exhibits the iso
363	via the `canonicalBaseChangeMap_isIso` helper. The deep mathematical
364	content (Stacks tag 02KH / 02KE / 00H8) lives entirely in the helper;
365	it reduces affine-locally to: for a flat ring map `A → B` and an
366	`A`-algebra `R`, the canonical map `B ⊗_A H^i(X, F) → H^i(X_B, F_B)`
367	is an iso for any quasi-coherent `F` (the `i = 0` form is what we use).
368	The helper remains a typed `sorry` pending the affine-local reduction
369	+ algebraic flat base change; this is iter-178+ body work after
370	quasi-compact open-cover Mayer-Vietoris infrastructure is in scope. -/
371	
372	/-- The canonical base-change natural transformation `g* (f_* -) ⟶ f'_* ((g')* -)`
373	associated to a cartesian square
374	```
375	     g'
376	X' ─────→ X
377	│         │

379	↓         ↓
380	S' ─────→ S

10	/-!
11	# The Hilbert polynomial of a coherent sheaf along a fibre (Nitsure §1)
12	
13	This file gives the **real definition** of the Hilbert polynomial
14	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.hilbertPolynomial` of a sheaf of modules `F` on
15	`X ⟶ S` at a point `s : S` relative to a line bundle `L`, via the graded
16	Hilbert function of the fibre section module — the `H⁰`-only encoding pinned
17	in the blueprint (`def:hilbert_polynomial`,
18	`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`):
19	
20	* the fibre `X_s = π.fiber s` is Mathlib's scheme-theoretic fibre
21	  `pullback π (S.fromSpecResidueField s)`;
22	* the restrictions `F_s`, `L_s` are the module pullbacks along the fibre
23	  embedding `π.fiberι s` (`Scheme.Hom.fiberModule`);
24	* the twists `F_s ⊗ L_s^{⊗m}` are `Scheme.Modules.moduleTensorPow` of
25	  `AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.SectionGradedRing`;
26	* their global sections are `κ(s)`-vector spaces via the structural ring
27	  homomorphism `κ(s) →+* Γ(X_s, 𝒪)` (`Scheme.Hom.fiberResidueMap`,
28	  `Scheme.Hom.fiberSectionsModule`), giving the graded Hilbert function
29	  `m ↦ dim_{κ(s)} Γ(X_s, F_s ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})` (`Scheme.hilbertFunction`);
30	* `Scheme.hilbertPolynomial π L F s` is the unique `Φ ∈ ℚ[λ]` that agrees
31	  with the graded Hilbert function for all `m ≫ 0` when such a polynomial
32	  exists, and the junk value `0` otherwise.  Whenever the eventual-match
33	  condition holds the polynomial is unique (two rational polynomials agreeing
34	  at all large naturals are equal), so the classical-choice extraction is
35	  canonical; no numerical invariant is lost (see the Serre-vanishing
36	  discussion in `def:hilbert_polynomial`).
37	
38	The bridge from the graded Hilbert–Serre rationality engine is
39	`Scheme.existsUnique_hilbertPolynomial_of_isRatHilb`: when the graded Hilbert
40	function is a rational Hilbert function (`AlgebraicGeometry.IsRatHilb`, the
41	output shape of `AlgebraicGeometry.gradedModule_hilbertSeries_rational`), the
42	eventual-match condition holds, the matching polynomial is unique, and
43	`hilbertPolynomial` computes it (`Scheme.hilbertPolynomial_eq_hilbertPoly`).
44	Producing the `IsRatHilb` hypothesis for proper `X_s` and ample `L_s` is the
45	finite-generation theorem `lem:sectionGradedModule_fg` (Serre), which is
46	future work; it enters only through the lemmas here, never through the
47	definition of `hilbertPolynomial` itself.
48	
49	Blueprint: `def:hilbert_polynomial`, `sec:graded_hilbert_polynomial`
50	(`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`).
51	Source: [Nitsure], §1, sub-section "Stratification by Hilbert Polynomials"
52	(`references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex`, L453–L478);
53	cf. [Hartshorne], III.5.2.

68	/-- The restriction `F_s = F|_{X_s}` of a sheaf of modules `F` on `X` to the
69	scheme-theoretic fibre `X_s = π.fiber s` of `π : X ⟶ S` over `s : S`: the
70	module pullback along the fibre embedding `π.fiberι s : π.fiber s ⟶ X`.
71	Blueprint: the restriction `F_s` of `def:hilbert_polynomial`. -/
72	noncomputable def Hom.fiberModule (π : X ⟶ S) (s : S) (F : X.Modules) :
73	    (π.fiber s).Modules :=
74	  (Scheme.Modules.pullback (π.fiberι s)).obj F
75	
76	/-- The structural ring homomorphism `κ(s) ⟶ Γ(X_s, 𝒪_{X_s})` of the fibre
77	`X_s = π.fiber s`: global sections of the structural morphism
78	`π.fiberToSpecResidueField s : X_s ⟶ Spec κ(s)`, transported along the
79	canonical isomorphism `Γ(Spec κ(s), ⊤) ≅ κ(s)`.  It makes every section
80	module of a sheaf of modules on the fibre a `κ(s)`-vector space
81	(`Scheme.Hom.fiberSectionsModule`). -/
82	noncomputable def Hom.fiberResidueMap (π : X ⟶ S) (s : S) :
83	    S.residueField s ⟶ Γ(π.fiber s, ⊤) :=
84	  (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (S.residueField s)).inv ≫ (π.fiberToSpecResidueField s).appTop
85	
86	/-- The `κ(s)`-module structure on the global sections of a sheaf of modules
87	`G` on the fibre `X_s = π.fiber s`: restriction of scalars along the
88	structural ring homomorphism `κ(s) →+* Γ(X_s, 𝒪)`
89	(`Scheme.Hom.fiberResidueMap`).  Not an instance (the fibre presentation of
90	the scheme is not canonical); brought into scope with `letI` at use sites. -/
91	@[reducible] noncomputable def Hom.fiberSectionsModule (π : X ⟶ S) (s : S)
92	    (G : (π.fiber s).Modules) :
93	    Module (S.residueField s) Γ(G, ⊤) :=
94	  Module.compHom Γ(G, ⊤) (π.fiberResidueMap s).hom
95	
96	/-- The **graded Hilbert function** of `F` at the fibre over `s : S`, relative
97	to the line bundle `L`:
98	
99	`m ↦ dim_{κ(s)} Γ(X_s, F_s ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})`,
100	
101	the dimension over the residue field `κ(s)` of the global sections of the
102	`m`-th twist of the restriction `F_s` by `L_s` on the scheme-theoretic fibre
103	`X_s = π.fiber s`.  This is the graded Hilbert function of the section graded
104	module `M(X_s, F_s, L_s)` (`def:sectionGradedModule`); it uses only `H⁰`.
105	When the dimension is not finite, `Module.finrank` takes the junk value `0`.
106	Blueprint: the graded Hilbert function of `def:hilbert_polynomial`. -/
107	noncomputable def hilbertFunction (π : X ⟶ S) (L F : X.Modules) (s : S)
108	    (m : ℕ) : ℕ :=
109	  letI := π.fiberSectionsModule s
110	    (Scheme.Modules.moduleTensorPow (π.fiberModule s F) (π.fiberModule s L) m)
111	  Module.finrank (S.residueField s)
112	    Γ(Scheme.Modules.moduleTensorPow (π.fiberModule s F) (π.fiberModule s L) m, ⊤)
113	
114	/-- Two rational polynomials that agree at all sufficiently large natural
115	arguments are equal: the agreement set is infinite, so the difference has
116	infinitely many roots. -/
117	private lemma eq_of_eval_nat_eventually_eq {Φ Ψ : Polynomial ℚ} {N₁ N₂ : ℕ}
118	    {f : ℕ → ℚ} (h₁ : ∀ m : ℕ, N₁ < m → Φ.eval (m : ℚ) = f m)
119	    (h₂ : ∀ m : ℕ, N₂ < m → Ψ.eval (m : ℚ) = f m) : Φ = Ψ := by
120	  refine Polynomial.eq_of_infinite_eval_eq _ _ ?_
121	  apply ((Set.Ioi_infinite (max N₁ N₂)).image
122	    (Nat.cast_injective (R := ℚ)).injOn).mono
123	  rintro x ⟨n, hn, rfl⟩
124	  rw [Set.mem_Ioi, sup_lt_iff] at hn
125	  exact Set.mem_setOf_eq ▸ (h₁ n hn.1).trans (h₂ n hn.2).symm

128	/-- The **Hilbert polynomial** of a sheaf of modules `F` on `X` over `S` at
129	the fibre over `s ∈ S`, relative to the line bundle `L`
130	(`def:hilbert_polynomial`, [Nitsure] §1; cf. [Hartshorne] III.5.2): the
131	unique polynomial `Φ_{F,s} ∈ ℚ[λ]` that agrees, for all `m ≫ 0`, with the
132	graded Hilbert function
133	
134	`m ↦ dim_{κ(s)} Γ(X_s, F_s ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})`
135	
136	of the fibre section module (`Scheme.hilbertFunction`), when such a
137	polynomial exists — and the junk value `0` otherwise.  Uniqueness holds
138	unconditionally (two polynomials agreeing at all large naturals are equal,
139	`Scheme.hilbertPolynomial_eq_of_eventually`), so the extraction is canonical.
140	
141	For proper `X_s`, ample `L_s` and coherent `F_s` the existence condition
142	holds (graded Hilbert–Serre; the `IsRatHilb`-conditional form is
143	`Scheme.existsUnique_hilbertPolynomial_of_isRatHilb`, and the remaining input
144	is the finite generation `lem:sectionGradedModule_fg`), and for `m ≫ 0` the
145	graded Hilbert function agrees with the Euler characteristic
146	`χ(X_s, F_s ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})` by Serre vanishing, so `Φ_{F,s}` is the classical
147	(Snapper) Hilbert polynomial — no numerical invariant is lost.  When `F` is
148	`S`-flat the function `s ↦ Φ_{F,s}` is locally constant on `S`. -/
149	noncomputable def hilbertPolynomial [IsLocallyNoetherian S] (π : X ⟶ S)
150	    [LocallyOfFiniteType π] (L F : X.Modules) (s : S) :
151	    Polynomial ℚ :=
152	  if h : ∃ Φ : Polynomial ℚ, ∃ N : ℕ, ∀ m : ℕ, N < m →
153	      Φ.eval (m : ℚ) = (hilbertFunction π L F s m : ℚ)
154	  then h.choose else 0

159	variable (L F : X.Modules) (s : S)

161	/-- **Well-definedness of the Hilbert polynomial**: any polynomial that agrees
162	with the graded Hilbert function `m ↦ dim_{κ(s)} Γ(X_s, F_s ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})` for
163	all `m ≫ 0` *is* `hilbertPolynomial π L F s`.  This is the uniqueness clause
164	of `def:hilbert_polynomial`. -/
165	theorem hilbertPolynomial_eq_of_eventually {Φ : Polynomial ℚ} {N : ℕ}
166	    (hΦ : ∀ m : ℕ, N < m → Φ.eval (m : ℚ) = (hilbertFunction π L F s m : ℚ)) :
167	    hilbertPolynomial π L F s = Φ := by
168	  have h : ∃ Φ' : Polynomial ℚ, ∃ N' : ℕ, ∀ m : ℕ, N' < m →
169	      Φ'.eval (m : ℚ) = (hilbertFunction π L F s m : ℚ) := ⟨Φ, N, hΦ⟩
170	  rw [hilbertPolynomial, dif_pos h]
171	  obtain ⟨N', hN'⟩ := h.choose_spec
172	  exact eq_of_eval_nat_eventually_eq hN' hΦ
173	
174	/-- **Defining property of the Hilbert polynomial**: if some polynomial
175	agrees with the graded Hilbert function for all `m ≫ 0`, then
176	`hilbertPolynomial π L F s` does. -/
177	theorem hilbertPolynomial_eval_eventually
178	    (h : ∃ Φ : Polynomial ℚ, ∃ N : ℕ, ∀ m : ℕ, N < m →
179	      Φ.eval (m : ℚ) = (hilbertFunction π L F s m : ℚ)) :
180	    ∃ N : ℕ, ∀ m : ℕ, N < m →
181	      (hilbertPolynomial π L F s).eval (m : ℚ) = (hilbertFunction π L F s m : ℚ) := by
182	  obtain ⟨Φ, N, hΦ⟩ := h
183	  refine ⟨N, fun m hm => ?_⟩
184	  rw [hilbertPolynomial_eq_of_eventually π L F s hΦ]
185	  exact hΦ m hm

188	omit [IsLocallyNoetherian S] [LocallyOfFiniteType π] in

190	the Hilbert series** (the polynomial-extraction step of
191	`thm:hilbertPoly_of_sectionModule`): if the graded Hilbert function of `F` at
192	`s` is a rational Hilbert function of some order `d`
193	(`AlgebraicGeometry.IsRatHilb`, the output of the graded Hilbert–Serre engine
194	`AlgebraicGeometry.gradedModule_hilbertSeries_rational`), then there is a
195	*unique* polynomial agreeing with it for all `m ≫ 0`.  Combines the
196	rationality datum with Mathlib's `Polynomial.existsUnique_hilbertPoly`
197	coefficient extraction (`lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib`). -/
198	theorem existsUnique_hilbertPolynomial_of_isRatHilb {d : ℕ}
199	    (hrat : IsRatHilb (fun m => (hilbertFunction π L F s m : ℚ)) d) :
200	    ∃! Φ : Polynomial ℚ, ∃ N : ℕ, ∀ m : ℕ, N < m →
201	      Φ.eval (m : ℚ) = (hilbertFunction π L F s m : ℚ) := by
202	  obtain ⟨p, N, hp⟩ := hrat
203	  refine ⟨Polynomial.hilbertPoly p d, ⟨max N p.natDegree, fun m hm => ?_⟩, ?_⟩
204	  · rw [← Polynomial.coeff_mul_invOneSubPow_eq_hilbertPoly_eval d
205	      (lt_of_le_of_lt (le_max_right _ _) hm)]
206	    exact (hp m (lt_of_le_of_lt (le_max_left _ _) hm)).symm
207	  · rintro Ψ ⟨N', hΨ⟩
208	    refine eq_of_eval_nat_eventually_eq hΨ
209	      (f := fun m => (hilbertFunction π L F s m : ℚ)) (N₂ := max N p.natDegree)
210	      (fun m hm => ?_)
211	    rw [← Polynomial.coeff_mul_invOneSubPow_eq_hilbertPoly_eval d
212	      (lt_of_le_of_lt (le_max_right _ _) hm)]
213	    exact (hp m (lt_of_le_of_lt (le_max_left _ _) hm)).symm

217	the defining eventual-agreement property with the graded Hilbert function.
218	This is the conditional content of `thm:hilbertPoly_of_sectionModule`; the
219	geometric input producing `IsRatHilb` (properness of `X_s` + ampleness of

223	    ∃ N : ℕ, ∀ m : ℕ, N < m →
224	      (hilbertPolynomial π L F s).eval (m : ℚ) = (hilbertFunction π L F s m : ℚ) :=

226	    (existsUnique_hilbertPolynomial_of_isRatHilb π L F s hrat).exists

\end{proof}

\section{Support and freeness predicates}
\label{sec:quot_predicates}

The Quot functor (\cref{def:quot_functor}) and the Grassmannian
(\cref{def:grassmannian_scheme}) classify quotient sheaves subject to two
geometric conditions: a coherent quotient must have \emph{schematic support
proper over the base}, and the Grassmannian's quotients must be \emph{locally
free of a fixed rank}. This section introduces both predicates.

\begin{definition}

\leanok
[Annihilator ideal sheaf of a sheaf of modules]
  \label{def:modules_annihilator}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.annihilator}
  % NOTE: The definition carries no \uses: the Lean construction is
  % `IdealSheafData.ofIdeals (fun U => Module.annihilator Γ(X,U) Γ(F,U))`, which is well-defined
  % unconditionally and does NOT depend on the localization bridges at definition time. The two
  % facts lem:annihilator_localization_eq_map and lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen are needed
  % only for the downstream CHARACTERIZATION (the reverse inclusion / equality on affine opens, the
  % "annihilator forward direction"), which is not yet a blueprint block.
  % NOTE: The definition itself is formalized and axiom-clean; the inclusion direction
  % \(\mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})(U) \subseteq \mathrm{Ann}_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)}(\mathcal{F}(U))\)
  % (\cref{lem:modules_annihilator_ideal_le}) is closed. The reverse inclusion, which identifies
  % the annihilator sheaf with the module-annihilator on affine opens, remains bridge-gated on
  % \cref{lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen}.
  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Stratification by Hilbert
  % Polynomials" (read from
  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L468-L471).
  % SOURCE QUOTE:
  % "Let $X\to S$ be a finite type morphism of noetherian schemes,
  %  and let $L$ be a line bundle on $X$. Let $\F$
  %  be any coherent sheaf on
  %  $X$ whose schematic support is proper over $S$."
  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
  Let \(X\) be a scheme and let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a sheaf of
  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules that is \emph{quasi-coherent and of finite type}
  (over a locally noetherian base, so that on each affine open \(U\) the section
  module \(\mathcal{F}(U)\) is a finitely generated \(\mathcal{O}_X(U)\)-module).
  The \emph{annihilator ideal sheaf} of \(\mathcal{F}\) is the ideal sheaf
  \(\mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})\) defined on affine opens: for each affine open
  \(U \subseteq X\),
  \[
    \mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})(U)
    \;=\; \mathrm{Ann}_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)}\bigl(\mathcal{F}(U)\bigr)
    \;\subseteq\; \mathcal{O}_X(U),
  \]
  the annihilator of the \(\mathcal{O}_X(U)\)-module \(\mathcal{F}(U)\). These
  local data patch to a coherent ideal sheaf on \(X\) whose vanishing locus is
  the set-theoretic support of \(\mathcal{F}\).

  \emph{Well-definedness on basic opens.} The coherence condition for an ideal
  sheaf requires that, for an affine open \(U\) and \(f \in \mathcal{O}_X(U)\),
  the section \(\mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})(U)\) restricts correctly to the basic
  open \(D(f) \subseteq U\), i.e. that
  \(\mathrm{Ann}_{\mathcal{O}_X(D(f))}(\mathcal{F}(D(f)))\) is the extension of
  \(\mathrm{Ann}_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)}(\mathcal{F}(U))\) along the localization map
  \(\mathcal{O}_X(U) \to \mathcal{O}_X(D(f))\). This is exactly where the two
  hypotheses enter. The bridge
  \cref{lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen} supplies, for a quasi-coherent
  finite-type \(\mathcal{F}\), that \(\mathcal{O}_X(D(f)) = (\mathcal{O}_X(U))_f\)
  and that the restriction \(\mathcal{F}(U) \to \mathcal{F}(D(f))\) exhibits
  \(\mathcal{F}(D(f))\) as the localized module \((\mathrm{powers}\,f)^{-1}
  \mathcal{F}(U)\); the algebra engine
  \cref{lem:annihilator_localization_eq_map} then yields, using that
  \(\mathcal{F}(U)\) is finitely generated, the required identity
  \(\mathrm{Ann}_{(\mathcal{O}_X(U))_f}\bigl((\mathrm{powers}\,f)^{-1}
  \mathcal{F}(U)\bigr) = \mathrm{Ann}_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)}(\mathcal{F}(U)) \cdot
  (\mathcal{O}_X(U))_f\). Thus the local annihilators are compatible with basic-open
  restriction, which is the patching datum that makes \(\mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})\)
  a well-defined ideal sheaf. The finite-type hypothesis is genuinely required:
  without finite generation of \(\mathcal{F}(U)\) the annihilator need not commute
  with localization, and the local data fail to patch.

  \emph{Note.} Mathlib does not carry an annihilator for sheaves of modules on a scheme.
\end{definition}

\begin{lemma}\leanok
[Annihilator ideal bounded by the module annihilator on affine opens]
  \label{lem:modules_annihilator_ideal_le}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.annihilator_ideal_le}
  \uses{def:modules_annihilator}
  Let \(X\) be a scheme, \(\mathcal{F}\) a sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules and
  \(U \subseteq X\) an affine open. Then the section over \(U\) of the annihilator
  ideal sheaf \(\mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})\) (\cref{def:modules_annihilator}) is
  contained in the module-annihilator of the sections of \(\mathcal{F}\) over
  \(U\):
  \[
    \mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})(U)
    \;\subseteq\; \mathrm{Ann}_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)}\bigl(\mathcal{F}(U)\bigr).
  \]
  This is the inclusion direction available directly from the definition of
  \(\mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})\); the reverse inclusion is the basic-open coherence
  statement gated on \cref{lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen}. Proved directly
  in Lean. It supports the well-definedness of the schematic support
  (\cref{def:schematic_support}).
\end{lemma}


\begin{lemma}[Sections on a basic open localize the affine ring]
  \label{lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_basicOpen}
  \mathlibok
  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
  (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme}).}
  Let \(X\) be a scheme, \(U \subseteq X\) an affine open, and
  \(f \in \Gamma(X, U)\). Then the restriction map
  \(\Gamma(X, U) \to \Gamma\bigl(X,\, D(f)\bigr)\) on the basic open
  \(D(f) = X.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,f\) exhibits \(\Gamma(X, D(f))\) as the
  localization of \(\Gamma(X, U)\) away from \(f\); that is, it is
  \(\mathrm{IsLocalization.Away}\,f\), so
  \(\Gamma(X, D(f)) = (\Gamma(X, U))_f\) as a \(\Gamma(X,U)\)-algebra.
\end{lemma}


% --- gap-2 supporting infrastructure (the named pieces of the gap-2 transport above) ---

\begin{lemma}[Annihilator of a finitely generated module commutes with localization]
  \label{lem:annihilator_localization_eq_map}
  \lean{Module.annihilator_isLocalizedModule_eq_map}
  \leanok
  % SOURCE: Stacks, Algebra, section "Supports and annihilators",
  % \texttt{lemma-annihilator-flat-base-change} (read from
  % references/stacks-algebra.tex, L9349-L9368): for a flat ring map R -> S and a
  % finite R-module M, Ann_R(M)S = Ann_S(M ⊗_R S). Localization at a submonoid is
  % the special case S = R_S, M ⊗_R R_S = M_S.
  \textit{Source: Stacks, Algebra, Lemma \texttt{annihilator-flat-base-change}
  (localization instance for finite modules).}
  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring, \(S \subseteq R\) a multiplicative submonoid, and
  \(R_S\) the localization of \(R\) at \(S\). Let \(M\) be a \emph{finitely
  generated} \(R\)-module and let \(g : M \to M_S\) exhibit \(M_S\) as the
  localization of \(M\) at \(S\) (an \(R_S\)-module whose \(R\)-action is the
  restriction of the \(R_S\)-action along \(R \to R_S\)). Then
  \[
    \mathrm{Ann}_{R_S}(M_S) \;=\; \mathrm{Ann}_R(M) \cdot R_S,
  \]
  the extension of the annihilator ideal along the canonical map
  \(R \to R_S\). Finite generation is essential: for a general module only the
  inclusion \(\supseteq\) holds.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \leanok
  Fix a finite generating set \(m_1, \dots, m_n\) of \(M\). An element of \(R\)
  annihilating every \(m_i\) annihilates all of \(M\): the elements it kills form
  a submodule (they are closed under sums and scalar multiples), and that
  submodule contains the generators.

  (\(\supseteq\)) It suffices that the image of \(\mathrm{Ann}_R(M)\)
  annihilates \(M_S\). Every element of \(M_S\) has the form \(g(m)/s\) with
  \(m \in M\), \(s \in S\); for \(a \in \mathrm{Ann}_R(M)\),
  \((a/1)\cdot(g(m)/s) = g(a \cdot m)/s = 0\).

  (\(\subseteq\)) Let \(y \in \mathrm{Ann}_{R_S}(M_S)\) and write \(y = a/s\)
  with \(a \in R\), \(s \in S\). For each \(i\) the hypothesis gives
  \(y \cdot (g(m_i)/1) = g(a \cdot m_i)/s = 0\) in \(M_S\), so there is
  \(u_i \in S\) with \(u_i \cdot a \cdot m_i = 0\) in \(M\). Put
  \(u := u_1 \cdots u_n \in S\); since each \(u_i\) divides \(u\) in \(S\),
  \((u a) \cdot m_i = 0\) for every \(i\), hence
  \(u a \in \mathrm{Ann}_R(M)\) by the generating-set criterion. Finally
  \[
    \frac{a}{s} \;=\; \frac{ua}{1} \cdot \frac{1}{us}
  \]
  exhibits \(y\) as an \(R_S\)-multiple of the image of
  \(ua \in \mathrm{Ann}_R(M)\), so \(y \in \mathrm{Ann}_R(M) \cdot R_S\).
\end{proof}

% --- Pullback of a quasi-coherent sheaf along an open immersion is quasi-coherent ---


\begin{lemma}[Sheaf Mayer--Vietoris: the pullback cone over a two-open cover is a limit]
  \label{lem:isLimitPullbackCone_mathlib}
  \lean{TopCat.Sheaf.isLimitPullbackCone}
  \mathlibok
  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
  (\texttt{Mathlib.Topology.Sheaves.SheafCondition.PairwiseIntersections}).}
  For a sheaf \(F\) on a topological space and two opens \(U, V\), the square relating
  \(F(U \cup V)\), \(F(U)\), \(F(V)\), \(F(U \cap V)\) is a pullback (limit). This is the generic
  module Mayer--Vietoris used in the inductive step of the descent.
\end{lemma}

\label{def:is_locally_free_of_rank}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank}
  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, "Elementary Examples, Exercises (2)" and
  % "Grassmannian of a vector bundle" (read from
  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L562-L564
  % + L584-L585).
  % SOURCE QUOTE:
  % "$\langle \F, q\rangle$ of quotients $q: \oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_T \to \F$
  %  where $\F$ is a locally free sheaf on $T$ of rank $d$.
  %  [...]
  %  the set of all equivalence classes $\langle \F, q\rangle$ of quotients
  %  $q: E_T \to \F$ where $\F$ is a locally free sheaf on $T$ of rank $d$,"
  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1, Exercise (2) (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
  Let \(\mathcal{M}\) be a sheaf of modules on a scheme \(X\) and let
  \(d \in \mathbb{N}\). We say \(\mathcal{M}\) is \emph{locally free of rank
  \(d\)} when there exists an open cover \(\{U_i\}\) of \(X\) together with
  isomorphisms \(\mathcal{M}|_{U_i} \cong \mathcal{O}_{U_i}^{\oplus d}\) for
  each \(i\).

  \emph{Note.} Mathlib does not provide this predicate.
\end{definition}

\section{The Quot functor}
\label{sec:quot_functor}

\begin{definition}\leanok
[Quot functor]
  \label{def:quot_functor}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotFunctor}
  \uses{def:hilbert_polynomial, def:has_proper_support}

  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "The Functors \(\Hilb_{X/S}\) and
  % \(\Quot_{E/X/S}\)" (read from
  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L394-L433
  % + L481-L498).
  % SOURCE QUOTE:
  % "Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme and let \(X\to S\) be a finite type
  %  scheme over it. Let \(E\) be a coherent sheaf on \(X\).
  %  Let \(Sch_S\) denote the category of all locally noetherian schemes
  %  over \(S\). For any \(T\to S\) in \(Sch_S\), a \textbf{family of quotients of \(E\)
  %  parametrised by \(T\)} will mean
  %  a pair \((\F,q)\) consisting of
  %
  %  (i) a coherent sheaf \(\F\) on \(X_T = X\times_ST\)
  %  such that the schematic support of \(\F\) is proper over \(T\)
  %  and \(\F\) is flat over \(T\), together with
  %
  %  (ii) a surjective \({\mathcal O}_{X_T}\)-linear homomorphism of sheaves
  %  \(q:E_T  \to \F\) where \(E_T\) is the pull-back of \(E\) under
  %  the projection \(X_T \to X\).
  %
  %  Two such families \((\F,q)\) and \((\F,q)\) parametrised by \(T\) will
  %  be regarded as equivalent if \(\ker(q) = \ker(q')\)),
  %  and \(\langle \F, q \rangle\) will denote
  %  an equivalence class. Then as properness and flatness are preserved
  %  by base-change, and as tensor-product is right exact,
  %  the pull-back of \(\langle \F, q \rangle\) under an \(S\)-morphism \(T'\to T\) is
  %  well-defined, which gives
  %  a set-valued contravariant functor
  %  \(\Quot_{E/X/S} : Sch_S \to Sets\) under which
  %  \(\)T\mapsto  \{\mbox{ All } \langle \F, q \rangle \mbox{ parametrised by }T\}\(\)
  %  [...]
  %  This shows that the functor \(\Quot_{E/X/S}\) naturally decomposes
  %  as a co-product
  %  \(\)\Quot_{E/X/S} = \coprod_{{\Phi}\in \Q[\lambda]}\, \Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}\(\)
  %  where for any polynomial \({\Phi}\in \Q[\lambda]\), the functor
  %  \(\Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}\) associates
  %  to any \(T\) the set of all equivalence classes of
  %  families \(\langle \F,q \rangle\) such that at each \(t\in T\) the
  %  Hilbert polynomial of the restriction \(\F_t\), calculated
  %  using the pull-back of \(L\), is \({\Phi}\)."
  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
  Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme, \(\pi : X \to S\) a finite-type morphism,
  \(L\) a line bundle on \(X\), \(E\) a coherent sheaf on \(X\), and
  \(\Phi \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]\). The \emph{Quot functor with Hilbert
  polynomial \(\Phi\)}
  \[
    \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S} : (\mathrm{Sch}/S)^{op} \longrightarrow \mathbf{Set}
  \]
  sends an \(S\)-scheme \(T \to S\) to the set of equivalence classes
  \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle\) of pairs \((\mathcal{F}, q)\) where
  \begin{enumerate}
    \item \(\mathcal{F}\) is a coherent sheaf on \(X_T = X \times_S T\) whose
      schematic support is proper over \(T\) (\cref{def:has_proper_support}) and
      which is flat over \(T\);
    \item \(q : E_T \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\) is a surjective
      \(\mathcal{O}_{X_T}\)-linear homomorphism, where \(E_T\) is the pull-back
      of \(E\) along \(X_T \to X\);
    \item for every \(t \in T\) the Hilbert polynomial of \(\mathcal{F}|_{X_t}\)
      with respect to \(L|_{X_t}\) (\cref{def:hilbert_polynomial}) equals
      \(\Phi\).
  \end{enumerate}
  Two pairs \((\mathcal{F}, q)\) and \((\mathcal{F}', q')\) are equivalent iff
  \(\ker(q) = \ker(q')\). On morphisms \(f : T' \to T\) over \(S\), the functor
  acts by pulling back the quotient along \(X_{T'} \to X_T\) (well-defined
  since tensor product is right-exact and preserves both flatness and
  properness). The Hilbert scheme is the special case
  \(\Hilb^{\Phi,L}_{X/S} := \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{\mathcal{O}_X/X/S}\).
\end{definition}

\section{The Grassmannian scheme}
\label{sec:grassmannian_scheme}

The Quot construction proceeds by embedding \(\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}\) as a
locally closed subfunctor of a Grassmannian over \(S\). Since Mathlib carries
no Grassmannian \emph{scheme} (only a finite-rank-quotient variety in
linear algebra), we package the construction here.

\begin{definition}\leanok
[Grassmannian scheme]
  \label{def:grassmannian_scheme}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian}
  \uses{def:quot_functor, def:is_locally_free_of_rank}
  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, "Elementary Examples, Exercises~(2)" and
  % "Construction of Grassmannian" (read from
  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L546-L599
  % + L773-L850).
  % SOURCE QUOTE:
  % "For any integers \(r\ge d\ge 1\), an explicit construction
  %  the Grassmannian scheme
  %  \(\grass(r,d)\) over \(\Z\), together with the tautological
  %  quotient \(u:\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\grass(r,d)} \to \U\) where \
  %  \(\U\) is a rank \(d\) locally free sheaf on \(\grass(r,d)\),
  %  has been given at the end of this section. [...]
  %  Show that \(\grass(r,d)\) together with the quotient
  %  \(u:\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\grass(r,d)} \to \U\) represents the
  %  contravariant functor
  %  \(\)\Grass(r,d) = \Quot_{\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\Z}/\Z/\Z}^{d, {\mathcal O}_{\Z}}\(\)
  %  from schemes to sets, which associates to any \(T\) the
  %  set of all equivalence classes
  %  \(\langle \F, q\rangle\) of quotients \(q: \oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_T \to \F\)
  %  where \(\F\) is a locally free sheaf on \(T\) of rank \(d\).
  %  [...]
  %  Using the above show that, more generally, if \(S\) is a scheme and
  %  \(E\) is a locally free \({\mathcal O}_S\)-module of rank \(r\),
  %  the functor \(\Grass(E,d)=\Quot_{E/S/S}^{d,{\mathcal O}_S}\)
  %  on all \(S\)-schemes which
  %  by definition associates to any \(T\)
  %  the set of all equivalence classes \(\langle \F, q\rangle\) of quotients
  %  \(q: E_T \to \F\) where \(\F\) is a locally free sheaf on \(T\) of rank \(d\),
  %  is representable. The representing scheme
  %  is denoted by \(\grass(E,d)\) and is called the rank \(d\)
  %  relative Grassmannian of \(E\) over \(S\)."
  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1, Exercise (2) and "Construction of
  Grassmannian" (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
  Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme and let \(V\) be a locally free
  \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-module of rank \(r\). For an integer \(1 \le d \le r\), the
  \emph{Grassmannian of rank-\(d\) quotients of \(V\)} is the functor
  \[
    \mathrm{Grass}(V, d) : (\mathrm{Sch}/S)^{op} \longrightarrow \mathbf{Set},
    \qquad
    T \longmapsto \{ \langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle :
    q : V_T \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F},\; \mathcal{F}
    \text{ locally free of rank } d \}
  \]
  with equivalence \(\ker(q) = \ker(q')\); the rank-\(d\) local freeness of
  \(\mathcal{F}\) is as in \cref{def:is_locally_free_of_rank}. Concretely,
  \(\mathrm{Grass}(V, d) = \Quot^{d,\mathcal{O}_S}_{V/S/S}\): the Quot functor
  (\cref{def:quot_functor}) in the special case \(X = S\), \(E = V\), constant
  Hilbert polynomial \(\Phi = d\).
\end{definition}

\begin{lemma}[Representability predicate]
  \label{lem:functor_is_representable_mathlib}
  \lean{CategoryTheory.Functor.IsRepresentable}
  \mathlibok
  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
  Let \(\mathcal{C}\) be a category and \(F : \mathcal{C}^{op} \to \mathbf{Set}\)
  a presheaf of sets. The predicate \(F.\mathrm{IsRepresentable}\) asserts that
  \(F\) is representable, i.e.\ that there exists an object \(Y\) of
  \(\mathcal{C}\) together with a natural bijection
  \(\mathrm{Hom}_{\mathcal{C}}(-, Y) \cong F\) (the Yoneda bijection).
\end{lemma}

\begin{theorem}\leanok
[Representability of the Grassmannian]
  \label{thm:grassmannian_representable}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian.representable}
  \uses{def:grassmannian_scheme, thm:relative_spec_exists, thm:relative_spec_univ,
    lem:functor_is_representable_mathlib, def:gr_glued_scheme, lem:gr_separated,
    lem:gr_proper}

  % NOTE (representability proof blocked): The proof sketch recovers the representing object
  % through the RepresentableBy Yoneda form via \cref{thm:relative_spec_univ}. However,
  % \cref{thm:relative_spec_univ} is currently established in Lean in a form weaker than a full
  % \(\mathrm{Functor.RepresentableBy}\) witness. The proof is blocked on either (a)
  % strengthening \cref{thm:relative_spec_univ} to deliver a RepresentableBy witness, or (b)
  % finding a RepresentableBy-free argument via direct gluing of chart-wise classifying morphisms.
  % This is a deferred open question.
  % NOTE: The Lean statement \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian.representable} is
  % currently a weakened existence skeleton that omits smoothness, properness, relative dimension
  % \(d(r-d)\), the tautological rank-\(d\) quotient, and the Pl\"ucker embedding. The
  % \verb|\lean{}| pin above points at a declaration that under-delivers the prose statement; it
  % should be strengthened or split into a separate skeleton label.

  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Construction by gluing together
  % affine patches" through "Projective embedding"
  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex,
  % L798-L930).
  % SOURCE QUOTE:
  % "For any integers \(r\ge d\ge 1\), the Grassmannian scheme
  %  \(\grass(r,d)\) over \(\Z\), together with the tautological
  %  quotient \(u:\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\grass(r,d)} \to \U\) where \
  %  \(\U\) is a rank \(d\) locally free sheaf on \(\grass(r,d)\),
  %  can be explicitly constructed as follows. [...]
  %  the schemes
  %  \(U^I\), as \(I\) varies over all the
  %  \({r\choose d}\) different
  %  subsets of  \(\{ 1,\ldots, r\}\) of cardinality \(d\),
  %  can be glued together by the co-cycle \((\theta_{I,J})\) to form a
  %  finite-type scheme \(\grass(r,d)\) over \(\Z\). As each \(U^I\) is
  %  isomorphic to \(\AA^{d(r-d)}_{\Z}\), it follows that
  %  \(\grass(r,d) \to \spec \Z\) is smooth of relative dimension \(d(r-d)\).
  %  [...]
  %  We now show that \(\pi : \grass(r,d) \to \spec \Z\) is proper. It is enough to
  %  verify the valuative criterion of properness for discrete valuation rings.
  %  [...]
  %  As \(\U\) is given by the transition functions \(g_{I,J}\) described above,
  %  the determinant line bundle
  %  \(\det(\U)\) is given by the transition functions
  %  \(\det(g_{I,J}) = 1/P^I_J \in GL_1(U^I_J)\).
  %  [...]
  %  We leave it to the reader to verify that the sections \(\sigma_I\) form
  %  a linear system which is base point free and separates points
  %  relative to \(\spec \Z\), and so gives an embedding of
  %  \(\grass(r,d)\) into \(\PP^m_{\Z}\) where \(m = {r\choose d}-1\).
  %  This is a closed embedding by the properness of
  %  \(\pi:\grass(r,d) \to \spec \Z\). In particular, \(\det(\U)\)
  %  is a relatively very ample line bundle on \(\grass(r,d)\) over \(\Z\)."
  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1, "Construction of Grassmannian" (FGA
  Explained Ch.~5).}
  Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme, \(V\) a locally free \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-module
  of rank \(r\), and \(1 \le d \le r\). The functor \(\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)\) of
  \cref{def:grassmannian_scheme} is representable by a smooth projective
  \(S\)-scheme \(\mathrm{Gr}_S(V, d) \to S\) of relative dimension \(d(r-d)\),
  equipped with a tautological quotient
  \(\pi^* V \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{U}\) of rank \(d\). The determinant
  line bundle \(\det(\mathcal{U})\) is relatively very ample, and the
  associated linear system gives a closed embedding
  \(\mathrm{Gr}_S(V, d) \hookrightarrow
  \mathbb{P}_S\!\left(\bigwedge^d V\right)\) -- the \emph{Pl\"ucker embedding}.

    (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp f g).hom.app N) T).hom w)
    hs3.symm).trans ?_

/-! ============================================================================
  UNION MERGE (2026-06-22, from subproject `GR-quot_closure`)

  Everything below this banner is the Grassmannian/Quot *quasi-coherent descent*
  development imported verbatim from the subproject's `Picard/QuotScheme.lean`
  (its post-`Grassmannian.representable` body). It is the rider machinery that
  the imported files `GrassmannianCells`, `GlueDescent`, `GrassmannianQuot`
  depend on (localized-module / quasicoherent descent: `isLocalizedModule_*`,
  `isIso_fromTildeΓ_*`, `annihilator`, `schematicSupport`, presentation-pullback
  machinery). The base-change cohomology lane above the banner is this project's
  own; both are preserved in full.
============================================================================ -/

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — Quot/Grassmannian predicates

These declarations build the support/freeness predicates of
`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`, §"Support and freeness
predicates". Mathlib (at the pinned commit) carries no rank-`d` local
freeness predicate for sheaves of modules on a scheme, so it is built here. -/

namespace SheafOfModules

/-- **Locally free of rank `d`** for a sheaf of modules on a scheme.

A sheaf of modules `M` on a scheme `X` is *locally free of rank `d`* when `X`
admits an open cover `{U i}` on each member of which the restriction
`M|_{U i}` (the pullback of `M` along the open immersion `(U i).ι`) is
isomorphic to the free module of rank `d`, `O_{U i}^{⊕ d}` (encoded as
`SheafOfModules.free (ULift (Fin d))` over the structure-ring sheaf of the
open subscheme `(U i).toScheme`).

This predicate is project-local: Mathlib does not supply a rank-indexed local
freeness predicate for sheaves of modules on a scheme. Blueprint:
`def:is_locally_free_of_rank` (Nitsure §1, Exercise (2)). -/
def IsLocallyFreeOfRank {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) (d : ℕ) : Prop :=
  ∃ (ι : Type u) (U : ι → X.Opens), (⨆ i, U i = ⊤) ∧
    ∀ i, Nonempty ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (U i).ι).obj M ≅
      _root_.SheafOfModules.free (R := (U i).toScheme.ringCatSheaf) (ULift.{u} (Fin d)))

end SheafOfModules

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — annihilator ideal sheaf and schematic support

These declarations build the annihilator ideal sheaf of a sheaf of modules and the
support/properness predicates of `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`,
§"Support and freeness predicates". Mathlib (at the pinned commit) carries no
annihilator ideal sheaf for sheaves of modules on a scheme, nor a schematic-support
or proper-support predicate, so they are built here.

The annihilator is packaged via `Scheme.IdealSheafData.ofIdeals`, exactly mirroring
Mathlib's `Scheme.Hom.ker` (which is `ofIdeals fun U ↦ RingHom.ker (f.app U).hom`):
`ofIdeals` produces *the largest ideal sheaf contained in* an arbitrary affine-local
family of ideals, so the structure's `map_ideal_basicOpen` coherence is discharged
internally and need not be supplied at definition time. The basic-open coherence that
makes the local annihilators agree with `ofIdeals` (the analogue of `Hom.ker_apply`,
`def:modules_annihilator`) is the separate characterization lemma `annihilator_ideal`,
which depends on the not-yet-closed QCoh→localization bridge
`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen` (`lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen`) together with
the algebra engine `Module.annihilator_isLocalizedModule_eq_map`
(`lem:annihilator_localization_eq_map`); see the handoff in
`task_results/.../QuotScheme.md`. -/

namespace Scheme.Modules

variable {X : Scheme.{u}}

/-- The **annihilator ideal sheaf** of a sheaf of modules `F` on a scheme `X`
(`def:modules_annihilator`).

On each affine open `U`, the intended section is the annihilator
`Ann_{Γ(X,U)}(Γ(F,U))` of the `Γ(X,U)`-module of sections `Γ(F,U)`. The ideal sheaf
is assembled with `Scheme.IdealSheafData.ofIdeals`, the largest ideal sheaf contained
in that affine-local family — exactly the construction used for `Scheme.Hom.ker`. This
sidesteps proving the basic-open coherence (`map_ideal_basicOpen`) at definition time;
the identity `(annihilator F).ideal U = Ann_{Γ(X,U)}(Γ(F,U))` is the downstream
characterization lemma (`annihilator_ideal`, blocked on the QCoh localization bridge).

This is a project-local primitive: Mathlib does not carry an annihilator ideal sheaf
for sheaves of modules on a scheme. -/
noncomputable def annihilator (F : X.Modules) : X.IdealSheafData :=
  IdealSheafData.ofIdeals fun U => Module.annihilator Γ(X, U.1) Γ(F, U.1)

/-- The component of the annihilator ideal sheaf at an affine open is contained in the
module annihilator of the sections. This is the always-available (`ofIdeals`) direction
of the characterization; the reverse inclusion is the basic-open coherence blocked on
`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen`. Project-local because `annihilator` is. -/
lemma annihilator_ideal_le (F : X.Modules) (U : X.affineOpens) :
    (annihilator F).ideal U ≤ Module.annihilator Γ(X, U.1) Γ(F, U.1) :=
  IdealSheafData.ideal_ofIdeals_le _ _

/-- The **schematic support** of a sheaf of modules `F` on a scheme `X`
(`def:schematic_support`): the closed subscheme of `X` cut out by the annihilator
ideal sheaf `annihilator F`. Project-local because `annihilator` is. -/
noncomputable def schematicSupport (F : X.Modules) : Scheme.{u} :=
  (annihilator F).subscheme

/-- The canonical closed immersion of the schematic support into the ambient scheme,
realizing `schematicSupport F` as a closed subscheme of `X` (`def:schematic_support`).
This is the `IdealSheafData.subschemeι` of the annihilator ideal sheaf; it is a
`IsPreimmersion` + `QuasiCompact` immersion onto the support. Project-local because
`annihilator` is. -/
noncomputable def schematicSupportι (F : X.Modules) : schematicSupport F ⟶ X :=
  (annihilator F).subschemeι

/-- The sheaf of modules `F` **has proper support over `S` along `f`**
(`def:has_proper_support`): the composite of the schematic-support immersion with
`f : X ⟶ S` is a proper morphism. Since `AlgebraicGeometry.IsProper` is stable under
base change, this condition is preserved by pullback, as required by the Quot functor's
pullback action. Project-local because `schematicSupport` is. -/
def HasProperSupport {S : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ S) (F : X.Modules) : Prop :=
  IsProper (schematicSupportι F ≫ f)

end Scheme.Modules

end AlgebraicGeometry

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — annihilator under localization

The annihilator ideal sheaf `def:modules_annihilator` of a coherent sheaf is
built from the affine-local data `U ↦ Ann_{O(U)}(F(U))`, packaged as a
`Scheme.IdealSheafData`. The structure's coherence field `map_ideal_basicOpen`

`Ann(S⁻¹M) = (Ann M)·S⁻¹R`. Mathlib (at the pinned commit) does not carry this

-/

annihilator commutes with localization: if `Rₚ` localizes `R` at a submonoid

namespace Scheme

/-! ## §1. Coherent-sheaf flatness over a base

For a morphism `f : X ⟶ S` of schemes and a coherent (more generally:
quasi-coherent) `𝓞_X`-module `𝓕`, the *coherent-sheaf flatness* predicate
`CoherentSheafFlat f 𝓕` captures Stacks 00HB lifted to the sheaf level:
for every point `x ∈ X` the stalk `𝓕_x` is a flat `𝓞_{S, f(x)}`-module
via the composite `𝓞_{S, f(x)} → 𝓞_{X, x} → End(𝓕_x)`.

Affine-locally (Stacks 00HB equivalence): on an affine open `U = Spec A
⊆ S` and an affine open `V = Spec B ⊆ f⁻¹U` with `𝓕|_V = M̃` for a
finite `B`-module `M`, the condition becomes `Module.Flat A M` with the
`A`-module structure inherited via the ring map `A → B`. The
file-internal encoding uses this affine-local form.

Blueprint reference: `def:coherent_sheaf_flat`. -/

/-- A coherent sheaf `𝓕` on `X` is **flat over `S`** for a morphism
`f : X ⟶ S` if, for every affine open `U ⊆ S` and every affine open
`V ⊆ f⁻¹U`, the `𝓞_X`-module of sections `Γ(𝓕, V)` is flat as a
`Γ(S, U)`-module, where the `Γ(S, U)`-action is the restriction of
scalars along the ring morphism `Γ(S, U) → Γ(X, V)` (the `appLE`
morphism of `f`).

This is the affine-local form of Stacks 00HB (a coherent sheaf is flat
over the base if every stalk is flat over the corresponding base
stalk); the affine-local form and stalk form are equivalent for
*quasi-coherent* sheaves on a noetherian base, which is the regime of
the flattening-stratification theorem.

iter-177+: the body of subsequent flat-locus theorems may package this
predicate as an instance-driven typeclass `Module.Flat`-style if the
sheafified-tensor infrastructure lands in Mathlib; for the iter-176
file-skeleton it is encoded as a `Prop` quantifier. -/
def CoherentSheafFlat {X S : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ S) (F : X.Modules) : Prop :=
  ∀ {U : S.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen U) {V : X.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen V)
    (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U),
    letI : Module Γ(S, U) Γ(F, V) := Module.compHom _ (f.appLE U V e).hom
    Module.Flat Γ(S, U) Γ(F, V)

end Scheme

/-! ## §2. Generic flatness (Nitsure §4) — see `GenericFlatnessGeometric.lean`

The geometric generic-flatness theorem `AlgebraicGeometry.genericFlatness`
(blueprint `thm:generic_flatness`) is stated and **proved** in the separate
file `AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.GenericFlatnessGeometric`, as the geometric
glue over the algebraic engine of this file: it consumes the qcqs
section-localization engine of `AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.QuotScheme`
(Stacks 01P0/01PC/01I8), which this file deliberately does not import so
that the instance environment of the dévissage proofs above stays fixed. -/

/-! ## §3–§6. Existence, universality, curve specialisation — moved

The geometric sections (Nitsure §4 sub-lemmas `flatLocusStratification` and
`flatLocusReduction`, the assembly `flatLocusAssembly`, the main theorem
`flatteningStratification`, its universal property, and the curve
specialisation `flatteningStratification.ofCurve`) live in
`AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.GenericFlatnessGeometric`: the proof of Lemma 6
(`flatLocusReduction`, run 0010 T12 r6) consumes `genericFlatness`, whose
geometric glue requires the QuotScheme engine that this file deliberately
does not import (see §2 above).  Blueprint `\lean{}` pointers are unchanged
— they pin fully qualified declaration names, which are preserved. -/

end AlgebraicGeometry
